headline: Vice President at Citi
about:
I am a Finance professional with more than 10 years of experience, I enjoy working within a diverse environment since I have interacted with partners in NY, India, Europe and Latam. Within the companies I have worked with I have been promoted given consistent performance. 

I am currently a Vice President within Citi's Financial Planning department (FP&A), in charge of Transformation reporting and restructuring for COO (Chief Operating Office). I also have experience leading the Earnings process for the Institutional Clients Group (ICG). For this, I interacted with Revenue leads, the Expense stream, the Cost of Credit lead, and the Balance Sheet group. My responsibility was to consolidate ICG's P&L to share early results with Corporate and the Controllers teams. To achieve this, I had to use different systems, manage large sets of data, and effectively communicate changes to all levels of seniority, including higher management (ICG's CFO in NY).

Complementing my day-to-day responsibilities, I joined ASDECITI's Board of Directors, as Treasurer and Investment Committee Lead. Within that position I co-managed an Investment Portfolio of ~$28mm and helped defining the best strategy to generate capital gains. ASDECITI is an Employee Association that manages funds from employee contributions (Costa Rica CSC) and seeks to invest them responsibly.

My work philosophy is to always seek process improvements while taking good care of stakeholder relations. I have also been exposed to international experience when I completed an Internship in Washington DC (2010), have traveled to New York, and completed a Stretch Assignment in India (2018). I have a US visa in place and don't need one to enter the European Union.

headline: Finance | Economist | Financial Modeling & Analysis
about:
As the Lead of Financial Planning and Analysis at César Iglesias S.A., I help shape the financial narrative of a company that made history as the first non-financial Dominican firm to go public. My role combines financial storytelling with data-driven strategy—leading budgeting processes, refining cash flow forecasting, and supporting investor relations through clear and timely reporting.

Previously, I worked in the Ministry of Finance of the Dominican Republic, where I developed macroeconomic models and collaborated with the IMF to strengthen fiscal frameworks. This experience sharpened my analytical lens and gave me a broader understanding of economic forces at both the sovereign and corporate level.

With a strong foundation in economics and a deep commitment to analytical rigor, I aim to keep evolving as a finance professional within dynamic, impact-driven organizations.
